heading: Vessel traffic services
label: 70001 Vessel traffic services

G, title LXXIII, § 7321(g)139 Stat. 1767Dec. 18, 2025“Not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating shall issue or amend such regulations as are necessary to address any applicable arrangements with the Canadian Coast Guard regarding vessel traffic services cooperation and vessel traffic management data exchanges within the Saint Lawrence Seaway and the Great Lakes.”Dec. 18, 2025

No Wake Zones in Navigation Channels, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–322, title I, § 1149130 Stat. 1660Pub. L. 116–260, div. AA, title III, § 347134 Stat. 2715Dec. 16, 2016Dec. 27, 2020“(a).—In GeneralAt the request of a State or local official, the Secretary [of the Army], in consultation with the Commandant of the Coast Guard, shall promptly identify and, subject to the considerations in subsection (b), allow the implementation of measures for addressing navigation safety hazards in a covered navigation channel resulting from wakes created by covered vessels identified by such official, while maintaining the navigability of the channel.“(b).—ConsiderationsIn identifying measures under subsection (a) with respect to a covered navigation channel, the Secretary shall consider, at a minimum, whether—“(1)State or local law enforcement officers have documented the existence of safety hazards in the channel that are the direct result of excessive wakes from covered vessels present in the channel;“(2)the Secretary has made a determination that safety concerns exist in the channel and that the proposed measures will remedy those concerns without significant impacts to the navigable capacity of the channel; and“(3)the measures are consistent with any recommendations made by the Commandant of the Coast Guard to ensure the safety of vessels operating in the channel and the safety of the passengers and crew aboard such vessels.“(c).—DefinitionsIn this section:“(1).—Covered navigation channelThe term ‘covered navigation channel’ means a navigation channel that—“(A)is federally marked or maintained;“(B)is part of the Atlantic Intracoastal Waterway; and“(C)is adjacent to a marina.“(2).—Covered vesselThe term ‘covered vessel’ means a recreational vessel or an uninspected passenger vessel, as such terms are defined in , United States Code.section 2101 of title 46“(d).—Savings ClauseNothing in this section shall be construed to relieve the master, pilot, or other person responsible for determining the speed of a vessel from the obligation to comply with the inland navigation regulations promulgated pursuant to section 3 of the Inland Navigational Rules Act of 1980 () or any other applicable laws or regulations governing the safe navigation of a vessel.”33 U.S.C. 2071

L. 109–347, title I, § 109120 Stat. 1893Oct. 13, 2006“(a).—Notice of ArrivalNot later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating shall update and finalize the rulemaking on notice of arrival for foreign vessels on the Outer Continental Shelf.Oct. 13, 2006“(b).—Content of RegulationsThe regulations promulgated pursuant to subsection (a) shall be consistent with information required under the Notice of Arrival under section 160.206 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations, as in effect on the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Oct. 13, 2006

children.text: in any port or place under the jurisdiction of the United States, in the navigable waters of the United States, or in any area covered by an international agreement negotiated pursuant to section 70005, may construct, operate, maintain, improve, or expand vessel traffic services, that consist of measures for controlling or supervising vessel traffic or for protecting navigation and the marine environment and that may include one or more of reporting and operating requirements, surveillance and communications systems, routing systems, and fairways;
shall require appropriate vessels that operate in an area of a vessel traffic service to utilize or comply with that service;

may require vessels to install and use specified navigation equipment, communications equipment, electronic relative motion analyzer equipment, or any electronic or other device necessary to comply with a vessel traffic service or that is necessary in the interests of vessel safety, except that the Secretary shall not require fishing vessels under 300 gross tons as measured under section 14502, or an alternate tonnage measured under section 14302 as prescribed by the Secretary under section 14104, or recreational vessels 65 feet or less to possess or use the equipment or devices required by this subsection solely under the authority of this chapter;

children.label: A
children.text: specifying times of entry, movement, or departure;

children.label: B
children.text: establishing vessel traffic routing schemes;

children.label: C
children.text: establishing vessel size, speed, or draft limitations and vessel operating conditions; and

children.label: D
children.text: restricting operation, in any hazardous area or under hazardous conditions, to vessels that have particular operating characteristics or capabilities that the Secretary considers necessary for safe operation under the circumstances;

may require the receipt of prearrival messages from any vessel, destined for a port or place subject to the jurisdiction of the United States, in sufficient time to permit advance vessel traffic planning before port entry, which shall include any information that is not already a matter of record and that the Secretary determines necessary for the control of the vessel and the safety of the port or the marine environment; and

may prohibit the use on vessels of electronic or other devices that interfere with communication and navigation equipment, except that such authority shall not apply to electronic or other devices certified to transmit in the maritime services by the Federal Communications Commission and used within the frequency bands 157.1875–157.4375 MHz and 161.7875–162.0375 MHz.

children.heading: Establishment and update of national policy.—
children.label: 1 Establishment and update of national policy.—

children.heading: .—Establishment of policy
children.label: A .—Establishment of policy
children.text: Not later than one year after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary shall establish a national policy which is inclusive of local variances permitted under subsection (c), to be applied to all vessel traffic service centers and publish such policy in the Federal Register.

children.heading: .—Update
children.label: B .—Update
children.text: The Secretary shall periodically update the national policy established under subparagraph (A) and shall publish such update in the Federal Register or on a publicly available website.

children.label: A
children.text: Standardization of titles, roles, and responsibilities for all personnel assigned, working, or employed in a vessel traffic service center.

children.label: B
children.text: Standardization of organizational structure within vessel traffic service centers, to include supervisory and reporting chain and processes.

children.label: C
children.text: Establishment of directives for the application of authority provided to each vessel traffic service center, specifically with respect to directing or controlling vessel movement when such action is justified in the interest of safety.

children.label: D
children.text: Establishment of thresholds and measures for monitoring, informing, recommending, and directing vessel traffic.

children.label: E
children.text: Establishment of national procedures and protocols for vessel traffic management.

children.label: F
children.text: Standardization of training for all vessel traffic service directors, operators, and watchstanders.

children.label: G
children.text: Establishment of certification and competency evaluation for all vessel traffic service directors, operators, and watchstanders.

children.label: H
children.text: Establishment of standard operating language when communicating with vessel traffic users.

children.label: I
children.text: Establishment of data collection, storage, management, archiving, and dissemination policies and procedures for vessel incidents and near-miss incidents.

children.heading: .—Development
children.label: 1 .—Development
children.text: In this section, the Secretary may provide for such local variances as the Secretary considers appropriate to account for the unique vessel traffic, waterway characteristics, and any additional factors that are appropriate to enhance navigational safety in any area where vessel traffic services are provided.

children.heading: .—Review and approval by secretary
children.label: 2 .—Review and approval by secretary
children.text: The Captain of the Port covered by a vessel traffic service center may develop and submit to the Secretary regional policies in addition to the national policy established and updated under subsection (b) to account for variances from that national policy with respect to local vessel traffic conditions and volume, geography, water body characteristics, waterway usage, and any additional factors that the Captain considers appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary shall review such regional policies; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Captain of the port concerned shall implement the policies that the Secretary approves.

children.heading: .—Maintenance
children.label: 4 .—Maintenance
children.text: The Secretary shall maintain a central depository for all local variances approved under this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter into cooperative agreements with public or private agencies, authorities, associations, institutions, corporations, organizations, or other persons to carry out the functions under subsection (a)(1).

children.heading: .—International coordination
children.label: 2 .—International coordination
children.text: With respect to vessel traffic service areas that cross international boundaries, the Secretary may enter into bilateral or cooperative agreements with international partners to jointly carry out the functions under subsection (a)(1) and to jointly manage such areas to collect, share, assess, and analyze information in the possession or control of the international partner.

children.heading: .—Inherently governmental function
children.label: A .—Inherently governmental function
children.text: A nongovernmental entity may not under this subsection carry out an inherently governmental function.

children.heading: .—Definition of inherently governmental function
children.label: B .—Definition of inherently governmental function
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “inherently governmental function” means any activity that is so intimately related to the public interest as to mandate performance by an officer or employee of the Federal Government, including an activity that requires either the exercise of discretion in applying the authority of the Government or the use of judgment in making a decision for the Government.

children.heading: .—Disclosure
children.label: 4 .—Disclosure
children.text: The Commandant of the Coast Guard shall de-identify information prior to release to the public, including near miss incidents.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop and implement a standard method for evaluating the performance of vessel traffic service centers.

children.label: A
children.text: Volume of vessel traffic, categorized by type of vessel.

children.label: B
children.text: Total volume of flammable, combustible, or hazardous liquid cargo transported, categorized by vessel type as provided in the Notice of Arrival, if applicable, or as determined by other means.

children.label: C
children.text: Data on near-miss incidents.

children.label: D
children.text: Data on marine casualties.

children.label: E
children.text: Application by vessel traffic operators of traffic management authority during near-miss incidents and marine casualties.

children.label: F
children.text: Other additional methods as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: recommendations to improve safety and performance; and

children.label: B
children.text: data regarding marine casualties and near-miss incidents that have occurred during the period covered by the report.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a continuous risk assessment program to evaluate and mitigate safety risks for each vessel traffic service area to improve safety and reduce the risks of oil and hazardous material discharge in navigable waters.

children.heading: .—Method for assessment
children.label: 2 .—Method for assessment
children.text: The Secretary, in coordination with stakeholders and the public, shall develop a standard method for conducting risk assessments under paragraph (1) that includes the collection and management of all information necessary to identify and analyze potential hazardous navigational trends within a vessel traffic service area.

children.label: i
children.text: Volume of vessel traffic, categorized by type of vessel.

children.label: ii
children.text: Total volume of flammable, combustible, or hazardous liquid cargo transported, categorized by vessel type as provided in the Notice of Arrival, if applicable, or as determined by other means.

children.label: iii
children.text: Data on near-miss events incidents.

children.label: iv
children.text: Data on marine casualties.

children.label: v
children.text: Geographic locations for near-miss events incidents and marine casualties, including latitude and longitude.

children.label: vi
children.text: Cyclical risk factors such as weather, ice cover, seasonal water body currents, tides, bathymetry, and topography.

children.label: vii
children.text: Weather data and information, in coordination with the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

children.heading: .—Information storage and management policies
children.label: B .—Information storage and management policies
children.text: The Secretary shall retain all information collected under subparagraph (A) and ensure policies and procedures are in place to standardize the format in which that information is retained to facilitate statistical analysis of that information to calculate within a vessel traffic service area, at a minimum, the incident rate, intervention rate, and casualty prevention rate.

children.heading: .—Assessments and information
children.label: A .—Assessments and information
children.text: In accordance with , the Secretary shall make any risk assessments conducted under paragraph (1) and any information collected under paragraph (3)(A) available to the public.section 552 of title 5

children.heading: .—Information in possession or control of international partners
children.label: B .—Information in possession or control of international partners
children.text: The Secretary shall endeavor to coordinate with international partners as described in subsection (d)(2) to enter into agreements to make information collected, shared, and analyzed under that paragraph available to the public.

children.heading: .—Disclosure
children.label: C .—Disclosure
children.text: The Commandant of the Coast Guard shall de-identify information prior to release to the public, including near-miss incidents.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a comprehensive nationwide training program for all vessel traffic service directors, operators, and watchstanders.

children.label: I
children.text: the national policy developed under subsection (b);

children.label: II
children.text: international rules under the International Navigational Rules Act of 1977 ( et seq.);33 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: III
children.text: inland navigation rules under part 83 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations;

children.label: IV
children.text: the application of vessel traffic authority; and

children.label: V
children.text: communication with vessel traffic service users.

children.label: ii
children.text: Proficiency training with respect to use, interpretation, and integration of available data on vessel traffic service display systems such as radar, and vessel automatic identification system feeds.

children.label: I
children.text: the international rules under the International Navigational Rules Act of 1977 ( et seq.); and33 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: II
children.text: the inland navigation rules under part 83 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.label: iv
children.text: Proficiency training with respect to the operation of radio communications equipment and any other applicable systems necessary to execute vessel traffic service authorities.

children.label: v
children.text: Incorporation of the Standard Marine Communication Phrases adopted by the International Maritime Organization by resolution on , as amended and consolidated, or any successor resolution.April 4, 2000

children.label: vi
children.text: Incorporation to the maximum extent possible of guidance and recommendations contained in vessel traffic services operator training, vessel traffic services supervisor training, or other relevant training set forth by the International Association of Marine Aids to Navigation and Lighthouse Authorities.

children.label: vii
children.text: A minimum number of hours of training for an individual to complete before the individual is qualified to fill a vessel traffic services position without supervision.

children.label: viii
children.text: Local area geographic and operational familiarization.

children.label: ix
children.text: Such additional components as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a standard competency qualification process to be applied to all personnel assigned, employed, or working in a vessel traffic service center.

children.heading: .—Application of process
children.label: B .—Application of process
children.text: The competency qualification process developed under subparagraph (A) shall include measurable thresholds for determining proficiency.

children.heading: International and inland navigation rules test.—
children.label: 3 International and inland navigation rules test.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: All personnel assigned, employed, or working in a vessel traffic service center with responsibilities that include communicating, interacting, or directing vessels within a vessel traffic service area, as determined under the national policy developed under subsection (b), shall be required to pass a United States international and inland navigation rules test developed by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Elements of test
children.label: B .—Elements of test
children.text: The Secretary shall determine the content and passing standard for the rules test developed under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Testing frequency
children.label: C .—Testing frequency
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a frequency, not to exceed once every 5 years, for personnel described in subparagraph (A) to be required to pass the rules test developed under such subparagraph.

children.heading: .—Vessel communication
children.label: 1 .—Vessel communication
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct research, in consultation with subject matter experts identified by the Secretary, to develop more effective procedures for monitoring vessel communications on radio frequencies to identify and address unsafe situations in a vessel traffic service area. The Secretary shall consider data collected under subparagraph (A) of subsection (f)(3).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct research, in consultation with local stakeholders and subject matter experts identified by the Secretary, to evaluate and determine the feasibility, costs and benefits of representation by professional mariners on the vessel traffic service watchfloor at each vessel traffic service center.

children.heading: .—Implementation
children.label: B .—Implementation
children.text: The Secretary shall implement representation by professional mariners on the vessel traffic service watchfloor at those vessel traffic service centers for which it is determined feasible and beneficial pursuant to research conducted under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Inclusion of Identification System on Certain Vessels.—
children.label: i Inclusion of Identification System on Certain Vessels.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The National Navigation Safety Advisory Committee shall advise and provide recommendations to the Secretary on matters relating to the practicability, economic costs, regulatory burden, and navigational impact of outfitting vessels lacking independent means of propulsion that carry flammable, combustible, or hazardous liquid cargo with vessel automatic identification systems.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: 2 .—Regulations
children.text: Based on the evaluation under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as the Secretary considers appropriate to establish requirements relating to the outfitting of vessels described in such subparagraph with vessel automatic identification systems.

children.heading: Periodic Review of Vessel Traffic Service Needs.—
children.label: j Periodic Review of Vessel Traffic Service Needs.—
A
children.text: if there are any additional vessel traffic service needs in those areas; and

children.label: B
children.text: if a vessel traffic service area should be moved or modified.

children.label: i
children.text: Volume of vessel traffic, categorized by type of vessel.

children.label: ii
children.text: Total volume of flammable, combustible, or hazardous liquid cargo transported, categorized by vessel type as provided in the Notice of Arrival, if applicable, or as determined by other means.

children.label: iii
children.text: Data on near miss incidents.

children.label: iv
children.text: Data on marine casualties.

children.label: v
children.text: Geographic locations for near-miss incidents and marine casualties, including latitude and longitude.

children.label: vi
children.text: Cyclical risk factors such as weather, ice cover, seasonal water body currents, tides, bathymetry, and topography.

children.label: vii
children.text: Weather data and information, in coordination with the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

children.heading: .—Stakeholder input
children.label: 3 .—Stakeholder input
children.text: In conducting the periodic reviews under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall seek input from port and waterway stakeholders to identify areas of increased vessel conflicts or marine casualties that could benefit from the use of routing measures or vessel traffic service special areas to improve safety, port security, and environmental protection.

children.heading: .—Disclosure
children.label: 4 .—Disclosure
children.text: The Commandant of the Coast Guard shall de-identify information prior to release to the public, including near miss incidents.

children.heading: Limitation of Liability for Coast Guard Vessel Traffic Service Pilots and Non-Federal Vessel Traffic Service Operators.—
children.label: k Limitation of Liability for Coast Guard Vessel Traffic Service Pilots and Non-Federal Vessel Traffic Service Operators.—

children.heading: .—Coast guard vessel traffic service pilots
children.label: 1 .—Coast guard vessel traffic service pilots
children.text: Any pilot, acting in the course and scope of his or her duties while at a Coast Guard Vessel Traffic Service Center, who provides information, advice, or communication assistance while under the supervision of a Coast Guard officer, member, or employee shall not be liable for damages caused by or related to such assistance unless the acts or omissions of such pilot constitute gross negligence or willful misconduct.

children.heading: .—Non-federal vessel traffic service operators
children.label: 2 .—Non-federal vessel traffic service operators
children.text: An entity operating a non-Federal vessel traffic information service or advisory service pursuant to a duly executed written agreement with the Coast Guard, and any pilot acting on behalf of such entity, is not liable for damages caused by or related to information, advice, or communication assistance provided by such entity or pilot while so operating or acting unless the acts or omissions of such entity or pilot constitute gross negligence or willful misconduct.

children.label: l
children.text: The regulations required under section 109(a) of the Security and Accountability For Every Port Act of 2006 ( note) dealing with notice of arrival requirements for foreign vessels on the Outer Continental Shelf shall not apply to a vessel documented under section 12105 unless the vessel arrives from a foreign port or place.46 U.S.C. 70001

children.heading: .—Existing Authority
children.label: m .—Existing Authority
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to alter the existing authorities of the Secretary to enhance navigation, vessel safety, marine environmental protection, and to ensure safety and preservation of life and property at sea.

children.heading: .—Hazardous liquid cargo
children.label: 1 .—Hazardous liquid cargo
children.text: The term “hazardous liquid cargo” has the meaning given that term in regulations prescribed under .section 5103 of title 49

children.heading: .—Marine casualty
children.label: 2 .—Marine casualty
children.text: The term “marine casualty” has the meaning given that term in regulations prescribed under section 6101(a).

children.heading: .—Vessel traffic service area
children.label: 3 .—Vessel traffic service area
children.text: The term “vessel traffic service area” means an area specified in subpart C of part 161 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation.

children.heading: .—Vessel traffic service center
children.label: 4 .—Vessel traffic service center
children.text: The term “vessel traffic service center” means a center for the provision of vessel traffic services in a vessel traffic service area.

children.heading: .—Near miss incident
children.label: 5 .—Near miss incident
children.text: The term “near miss incident” means any occurrence or series of occurrences having the same origin, involving one or more vessels, facilities, or any combination thereof, resulting in the substantial threat of a marine casualty.

children.heading: .—De-identified
children.label: 6 .—De-identified
children.text: The term “de-identified” means the process by which all information that is likely to establish the identity of the specific persons or entities noted in the reports, data, or other information is removed from the reports, data, or other information.
